name: Tuna Egg Potato Salad
purpose: Protein salad fallback or planned lunch.
instructions: If eggs are not prepared, boil eggs 9-10 minutes, then chill and peel.
Cut cooked potato into 2 cm pieces.
Mix yogurt, mustard, lemon juice, black pepper, and a small amount of salt.
In a bowl combine drained tuna, chopped eggs, potato, pickles, and herbs.
Add sauce and fold gently so the potatoes do not turn into mash.
Taste. Usually add more lemon/pepper before adding more salt.
Serve as a bowl or with 1-2 toasts if more energy is needed.
storage: Already mixed salad should be eaten within 24-36 hours.
Store components separately for better quality.
substitutions: A small amount of light mayo can be mixed with yogurt if desired.
meal_prep_notes: Keep tuna in cans, eggs boiled, potatoes separate, and sauce separate until serving.
why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Fast, high-protein, non-sweet fallback that uses pantry tuna and prepared potatoes.
